[Derwent College](/wiki/Derwent_College,_York "Derwent College, York"), [York](/wiki/University_of_York "University of York") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts"))

(born 30 May 1961) is an English comedian and actor. He is known in particular for his television work, including *[Harry Enfield's Television Programme](/wiki/Harry_Enfield_%26_Chums "Harry Enfield & Chums")*, *[Harry Enfield & Chums](/wiki/Harry_Enfield_%26_Chums "Harry Enfield & Chums")* and *[Harry & Paul](/wiki/Harry_%26_Paul "Harry & Paul")*, across which he created and portrayed comedy characters such as [Kevin the Teenager](/wiki/Kevin_the_Teenager "Kevin the Teenager"), [Loadsamoney](/wiki/Loadsamoney "Loadsamoney"), [Smashie and Nicey](/wiki/Smashie_and_Nicey "Smashie and Nicey"), [The Scousers](/wiki/The_Scousers "The Scousers"), Tim Nice-But-Dim and Mr "You Don't Want to Do It Like That".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actor, comedian and writer (born 1961)

| Harry Enfield | |
| --- | --- |
| Enfield in 2023 | |

| Education | [Dorset House School](/wiki/Dorset_House_School "Dorset House School")   [Worth School](/wiki/Worth_School "Worth School")   [Collyer's Sixth Form College](/wiki/The_College_of_Richard_Collyer "The College of Richard Collyer") |
| Alma mater | [Derwent College](/wiki/Derwent_College,_York "Derwent College, York"), [York](/wiki/University_of_York "University of York") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts")) |
| Spouse | Lucy Lyster  ​  ​ (m. 1997; sep. 2020)​ |

|  | |

| [Genres](/wiki/Comedic_genres "Comedic genres") | * [Sketch shows](/wiki/Sketch_shows "Sketch shows") * [character comedy](/wiki/Character_comedy "Character comedy") * satire |
|  | |

**Henry Richard Enfield** (born 30 May 1961) is an English comedian and actor. He is known in particular for his television work, including *[Harry Enfield's Television Programme](/wiki/Harry_Enfield_%26_Chums "Harry Enfield & Chums")*, *[Harry Enfield & Chums](/wiki/Harry_Enfield_%26_Chums "Harry Enfield & Chums")* and *[Harry & Paul](/wiki/Harry_%26_Paul "Harry & Paul")*, across which he created and portrayed comedy characters such as [Kevin the Teenager](/wiki/Kevin_the_Teenager "Kevin the Teenager"), [Loadsamoney](/wiki/Loadsamoney "Loadsamoney"), [Smashie and Nicey](/wiki/Smashie_and_Nicey "Smashie and Nicey"), [The Scousers](/wiki/The_Scousers "The Scousers"), Tim Nice-But-Dim and Mr "You Don't Want to Do It Like That".

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Enfield was born on 30 May 1961,[[1]](#cite_note-loadsa-1) in [Horsham](/wiki/Horsham "Horsham"), [Sussex](/wiki/Sussex "Sussex"),[[1]](#cite_note-loadsa-1) and is the eldest of four children (and only son) of English television, radio and newspaper journalist and presenter [Edward Enfield](/wiki/Edward_Enfield "Edward Enfield") and his wife, Deirdre Jenkins. The Enfield family are descendants of the nineteenth-century philanthropist [Edward Enfield](/wiki/Edward_Enfield_(philanthropist) "Edward Enfield (philanthropist)").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He spent his childhood living in [Billingshurst](/wiki/Billingshurst "Billingshurst"), West Sussex.[[1]](#cite_note-loadsa-1)

He was educated at the independent Arundale School in [Pulborough](/wiki/Pulborough "Pulborough"), [Dorset House School](/wiki/Dorset_House_School "Dorset House School"), [Worth School](/wiki/Worth_School "Worth School"), [Collyer's Sixth Form College](/wiki/The_College_of_Richard_Collyer "The College of Richard Collyer") (all in West Sussex) and the [University of York](/wiki/University_of_York "University of York"), where he was a member of [Derwent College](/wiki/Derwent_College,_York "Derwent College, York") and studied politics. He [squatted](/wiki/Squatting "Squatting") in Hackney and worked for a while as a [milkman](/wiki/Milkman "Milkman").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Enfield started his comedy career after being recruited at the [Edinburgh Fringe Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_Fringe_Festival "Edinburgh Fringe Festival"),[[1]](#cite_note-loadsa-1) as a writer for 3 episodes of the [Radio 4](/wiki/BBC_Radio_4 "BBC Radio 4") comedy programme *[Week Ending](/wiki/Week_Ending "Week Ending")* (1982-1983).[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5) In 1985, he took on a voice actor role alongside [Chris Barrie](/wiki/Chris_Barrie "Chris Barrie"), [Rory Bremner](/wiki/Rory_Bremner "Rory Bremner"), [Steve Coogan](/wiki/Steve_Coogan "Steve Coogan"), [Hugh Dennis](/wiki/Hugh_Dennis "Hugh Dennis"), and [Ade Edmondson](/wiki/Ade_Edmondson "Ade Edmondson") in the British [satirical](/wiki/Satirical "Satirical") television comedy [puppet show](/wiki/Puppet_show "Puppet show"), *[Spitting Image](/wiki/Spitting_Image "Spitting Image")* (1985–1989, 1996).[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5)

He captured wider public attention when appearing on [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4")'s *[Saturday Live](/wiki/Saturday_Live_(UK_TV_series) "Saturday Live (UK TV series)")* (1986-1988),[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) as several different characters created with [Paul Whitehouse](/wiki/Paul_Whitehouse "Paul Whitehouse"). These quickly entered the national consciousness. Among these characters were Stavros, a Greek kebab shop owner with fractured English; and [Loadsamoney](/wiki/Loadsamoney "Loadsamoney"), an obnoxious plasterer who constantly boasted about how much money he earned.[[1]](#cite_note-loadsa-1) The Loadsamoney character was created in reaction to the policies of the [Thatcher government](/wiki/Premiership_of_Margaret_Thatcher "Premiership of Margaret Thatcher") of the day, and took on a life of its own, sampling the songs "Money, Money" from the musical *[Cabaret](/wiki/Cabaret_(1972_film) "Cabaret (1972 film)")* and "[Money, Money, Money](/wiki/Money,_Money,_Money "Money, Money, Money")" by [ABBA](/wiki/ABBA "ABBA") to spawn a hit single in 1988 and a sell-out live tour.[[7]](#cite_note-7) In May 1988, [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") leader [Neil Kinnock](/wiki/Neil_Kinnock "Neil Kinnock") used the term *loadsamoney* to criticise the policies of the [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") government and journalists began to refer to the "loadsamoney mentality" and the "loadsamoney economy".[[8]](#cite_note-8)

As a foil to Loadsamoney, Enfield and Whitehouse created the [Geordie](/wiki/Geordie "Geordie") "Bugger-All-Money" and in 1988 Enfield appeared as both characters during the [Nelson Mandela 70th Birthday Tribute](/wiki/Nelson_Mandela_70th_Birthday_Tribute "Nelson Mandela 70th Birthday Tribute") Concert at Wembley Stadium. In time, Whitehouse and Enfield became disturbed that Loadsamoney was being seen in a positive light, rather than as a satirical figure, and they had him run over during a [Comic Relief Red Nose Day](/wiki/Comic_Relief "Comic Relief") show while leaving the studio after presenting host [Lenny Henry](/wiki/Lenny_Henry "Lenny Henry") with "the biggest cheque of the night"—a physically huge cheque for ten pence.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Enfield created "[Tory Boy](/wiki/Tory_Boy "Tory Boy")", a character which portrayed a young male [Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") (MP).

In 1989, Enfield realised a personal project, *[Norbert Smith - a Life](/wiki/Norbert_Smith_-_a_Life "Norbert Smith - a Life")*, a spoof on British theatrical knights "slumming" in the film industry. He starred as [Dirk Gently](/wiki/Dirk_Gently "Dirk Gently") in the [BBC Radio](/wiki/BBC_Radio "BBC Radio") adaptations of *[Dirk Gently's Holistic Detective Agency](/wiki/Dirk_Gently%27s_Holistic_Detective_Agency#BBC_Radio_adaptation "Dirk Gently's Holistic Detective Agency")* (1986-1987).[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5)

### TV series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: TV series")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/HarryEnfield1988.jpg/250px-HarryEnfield1988.jpg)](/wiki/File:HarryEnfield1988.jpg)

Enfield in 1998

In 1990, Enfield developed his [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") sketch show *[Harry Enfield's Television Programme](/wiki/Harry_Enfield_%26_Chums "Harry Enfield & Chums")*,[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5) later retitled *Harry Enfield & Chums*, with Whitehouse and [Kathy Burke](/wiki/Kathy_Burke "Kathy Burke"). Eschewing the [alternative comedy](/wiki/Alternative_comedy "Alternative comedy") style prevalent at the time, both versions of the show were indebted to comedians such as [Dick Emery](/wiki/Dick_Emery "Dick Emery") and [Morecambe and Wise](/wiki/Morecambe_and_Wise "Morecambe and Wise"). Enfield and his co-performers created another group of nationally recognised characters for these shows, such as *The Old Gits*, *Tim Nice-But-Dim*, *[The Scousers](/wiki/The_Scousers "The Scousers")*, *[Smashie and Nicey](/wiki/Smashie_and_Nicey "Smashie and Nicey")*, *Wayne and Waynetta Slob*, *Mr You-Don’t-Wanna-Do-It-Like-That*, *Annoying Kid Brother*, who grew into *[Kevin the Teenager](/wiki/Kevin_the_Teenager "Kevin the Teenager")*, and two old-fashioned presenters, *Mr Cholmondley-Warner and Grayson*.[[10]](#cite_note-hattrick-10)

In 1991, Enfield played Dermot in the [Thames Television](/wiki/Thames_Television "Thames Television") sitcom *[Men Behaving Badly](/wiki/Men_Behaving_Badly "Men Behaving Badly")* (1992),[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) alongside [Martin Clunes](/wiki/Martin_Clunes "Martin Clunes"), [Caroline Quentin](/wiki/Caroline_Quentin "Caroline Quentin") and [Leslie Ash](/wiki/Leslie_Ash "Leslie Ash"). Enfield left after the [first series](/wiki/List_of_Men_Behaving_Badly_episodes#Series_1_(1992) "List of Men Behaving Badly episodes"), and was replaced in the [second series](/wiki/List_of_Men_Behaving_Badly_episodes#Series_2_(1992) "List of Men Behaving Badly episodes") by [Neil Morrissey](/wiki/Neil_Morrissey "Neil Morrissey") as Tony.[[11]](#cite_note-wrong-11) Enfield is a professed fan of [opera](/wiki/Opera "Opera") and fronted a [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") documentary series on the subject. In 1991 Harry starred as Little Jim Morley in the series *[Gone to the Dogs](/wiki/Gone_to_the_Dogs_(TV_series) "Gone to the Dogs (TV series)")* (1991).[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5)

After a short break from television, Enfield signed a new contract with [BSkyB](/wiki/British_Sky_Broadcasting "British Sky Broadcasting"), but produced only one series, *[Harry Enfield's Brand Spanking New Show](/wiki/Harry_Enfield%27s_Brand_Spanking_New_Show "Harry Enfield's Brand Spanking New Show")*.[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5) In 2002 Enfield returned to the BBC as the ageing rockstar Gary Bloke in *Celeb*, which received poor audience ratings.[[11]](#cite_note-wrong-11)

In 2002, Enfield was the first guest on the revamped version of BBC's *[Top Gear](/wiki/Top_Gear_(2002_TV_series) "Top Gear (2002 TV series)")* where he did a 2 minute and 1 second lap.[[12]](#cite_note-12) He also appeared on the show on 23 November 2008.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Enfield has also narrated various TV documentaries, such as [Discovery Wings](/wiki/Discovery_Wings_(British_and_Irish_TV_channel) "Discovery Wings (British and Irish TV channel)")' *Classic British Aircraft*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In 2007, he played Jim Stonem in the Channel 4 series *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*.[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) He reprised this role in the second series in 2008, and the third series in 2009, and directed two episodes in season two entitled "Chris" and "Tony" in 2008.[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) He directed[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) and starred alongside [Paul Whitehouse](/wiki/Paul_Whitehouse "Paul Whitehouse") in their own comedy series *[Harry & Paul](/wiki/Harry_%26_Paul "Harry & Paul")* (originally titled *Ruddy Hell! It's Harry & Paul*) in 2007.[[15]](#cite_note-15) For their work on this series, they both jointly won the 2009 [British Academy Television Award for Best Comedy (Programme or Series)](/wiki/British_Academy_Television_Award_for_Best_Comedy_(Programme_or_Series) "British Academy Television Award for Best Comedy (Programme or Series)").[[16]](#cite_note-2009bafta-16)

In September 2013 Enfield appeared as Martin, the father of [Jack Whitehall](/wiki/Jack_Whitehall "Jack Whitehall")'s character Alfie, in the [BBC Three](/wiki/BBC_Three_(former) "BBC Three (former)") comedy series [*Bad Education*](/wiki/Bad_Education_(TV_series) "Bad Education (TV series)").[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) In October 2014, Enfield and Paul Whitehouse returned to the characters of Frank and George in a sketch for Channel 4's testicular cancer awareness comedy show "[The Feeling Nuts Comedy Night](/wiki/The_Feeling_Nuts_Comedy_Night "The Feeling Nuts Comedy Night")".[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In August 2015, Enfield, alongside Whitehouse, in celebration of their 25-year partnership, presented *An Evening With Harry Enfield and Paul Whitehouse*.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In 2016, he appeared as [John Shakespeare](/wiki/John_Shakespeare "John Shakespeare"), father of [William Shakespeare](/wiki/William_Shakespeare "William Shakespeare"), in the sitcom *[Upstart Crow](/wiki/Upstart_Crow "Upstart Crow")*.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

Since 2016, Enfield has appeared as [Prince Charles](/wiki/Charles_III_of_the_United_Kingdom "Charles III of the United Kingdom") (later [King Charles III](/wiki/Charles_III_of_the_United_Kingdom "Charles III of the United Kingdom")) in the [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") sitcom *[The Windsors](/wiki/The_Windsors "The Windsors")*.[[20]](#cite_note-20) For his portrayal of [Charles](/wiki/Charles_III_of_the_United_Kingdom "Charles III of the United Kingdom"), he was nominated for the [British Academy Television Award for Best Male Comedy Performance](/wiki/British_Academy_Television_Award_for_Best_Male_Comedy_Performance "British Academy Television Award for Best Male Comedy Performance") in 2017.[[21]](#cite_note-2017bafta-21)

### Films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Films")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Harry_Enfield_at_the_BAFTA%27s.jpg/250px-Harry_Enfield_at_the_BAFTA%27s.jpg)](/wiki/File:Harry_Enfield_at_the_BAFTA%27s.jpg)

Enfield in 2009

In 2000, Enfield appeared in his first leading film role playing Kevin alongside [Kathy Burke](/wiki/Kathy_Burke "Kathy Burke"), who played the character's (male) friend Perry—roles originally created for Enfield's television series—in *[Kevin & Perry Go Large](/wiki/Kevin_%26_Perry_Go_Large "Kevin & Perry Go Large")* (2000).[[5]](#cite_note-comedy-5) The film charted the pair's attempt to become professional DJs by travelling to the nightclubs of [Ibiza](/wiki/Ibiza "Ibiza") and pestering their idol, the DJ Eyeball Paul, played by [Rhys Ifans](/wiki/Rhys_Ifans "Rhys Ifans"), while gaining love and losing their virginity. Enfield also appeared as King [George VI](/wiki/George_VI "George VI") in *[Churchill: The Hollywood Years](/wiki/Churchill:_The_Hollywood_Years "Churchill: The Hollywood Years")* (2004),[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) a satire on Hollywood's tendency to change elements of history. In 2012, he starred with Simon Callow in the film *Acts of Gordfrey*.[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6)

He reprised his role as Martin in *The Bad Education Movie* (2015).[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6) He appeared as Bill in the 2015 film *[Scottish Mussel](/wiki/Scottish_Mussel "Scottish Mussel")*.[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6)

In 2015 Enfield and [Jessica Hynes](/wiki/Jessica_Hynes "Jessica Hynes") appeared as Mr and Mrs Jackson in a BBC film of the [Arthur Ransome](/wiki/Arthur_Ransome "Arthur Ransome") children's novel *[Swallows and Amazons](/wiki/Swallows_and_Amazons_(2016_film) "Swallows and Amazons (2016 film)")*.[[6]](#cite_note-tv-6)

In 2022, Enfield appeared as [Tony Blair](/wiki/Tony_Blair "Tony Blair") in the made-for-television biographical musical comedy film *[Prince Andrew: The Musical](/wiki/Prince_Andrew:_The_Musical "Prince Andrew: The Musical")*.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### Video games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Video games")]

In 2012, a likeness of his character Loadsamoney, named "Harold Lott", was released as a [DLC](/wiki/Downloadable_content "Downloadable content") skin for the game *[Killing Floor](/wiki/Killing_Floor_(video_game) "Killing Floor (video game)")*.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### Commercials

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Commercials")]

Enfield appeared in some television commercials before becoming famous, including one made in 1987 for [Tetley](/wiki/Tetley "Tetley"). Enfield's commercials include a series made in 1994 for [Dime Bar](/wiki/Dime_Bar "Dime Bar"). One commercial in this series had Enfield as a yokel refusing a Dime bar—smooth on the outside, crunchy on the inside—because he preferred [armadillos](/wiki/Armadillo "Armadillo")—smooth on the inside, crunchy on the outside. Later Enfield, with Paul Whitehouse, starred in a series of commercials for [Hula Hoops](/wiki/Hula_Hoops "Hula Hoops") as The Self-Righteous Brothers, characters from Enfield's television show. In 2004 Enfield starred in a series of commercials for [Burger King](/wiki/Burger_King "Burger King") in Paraguay as Dr Angus, a character intended to promote the company's newest hamburger. Two more characters from Enfield's TV series, Mr Cholmondley-Warner and Grayson, also appeared in commercials, for [Mercury Communications](/wiki/Mercury_Communications "Mercury Communications").[[24]](#cite_note-24) Also in 2004 Enfield provided the voice of "The Roaming Gnome" character used in [Travelocity](/wiki/Travelocity "Travelocity")'s U.S. advertising campaign. Enfield was also in commercials for Worthington Bitter.

### Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Music")]

Enfield made a cameo appearance as 'the tea lady' during [Blur](/wiki/Blur_(band) "Blur (band)")'s performance at the Olympics Closing Ceremony Celebration Concert in London's [Hyde Park](/wiki/Hyde_Park,_London "Hyde Park, London") in [2012](/wiki/London2012 "London2012").[[25]](#cite_note-25)

He also appeared alongside [Damon Albarn](/wiki/Damon_Albarn "Damon Albarn")'s other project [the Good, the Bad & the Queen](/wiki/The_Good,_the_Bad_%26_the_Queen "The Good, the Bad & the Queen"), as compere, during a live performance at the [Tower of London](/wiki/Tower_of_London "Tower of London"), on 9 July 2007.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

In January 2025, Enfield featured in *Ego Death*, a film of 10 interconnected music videos accompanying an album by [Archie Henderson](/wiki/Archie_Henderson_(comedian) "Archie Henderson (comedian)")'s musical persona, [Jazz Emu](/wiki/Jazz_Emu "Jazz Emu").[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28)

### Theatre

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Theatre")]

In 2021 Enfield starred as [Prince Charles](/wiki/Prince_Charles "Prince Charles") in *The Windsors: Endgame* at the [Prince of Wales Theatre](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales_Theatre "Prince of Wales Theatre") in London. The show ran for a limited time and closed on 9 October 2021.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

In August 2025 Harry Enfield announced his theatrical tour *Harry Enfield and \*No\* Chums*. Appearances were made November 2025 in both Australia and New Zealand.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Enfield&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 1997,[[32]](#cite_note-32) Enfield married Lucy Lyster; they have three children.[[33]](#cite_note-33) It was announced in August 2020 that the couple had separated.[[34]](#cite_note-34)
